message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  A small interfering RNA (siRNA) inhibitors have demonstrated the novel modality for suppressing infectious diseases. Sixty-one siRNA molecules, predicted by the bioinformatics programs, were screened for the possibility of treating severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 (SARS-CoV-2) using an
                  <jats:italic>in vitro</jats:italic>
                  plaque assay. Among six siRNA leads with the efficacy of reducing plaque number, the siRNA targeting RNA-dependent RNA polymerase (RdRp) showed a reduction in SARS-CoV-2 infection-induced fever and virus titer in the Golden Syrian hamster and rhesus macaque. These results suggest the potential for RdRp targeting siRNA as a new treatment for the coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19).
                </jats:p>
title: A Small interfering RNA lead targeting RNA-dependent RNA-polymerase effectively inhibit the SARS-CoV-2 infection in Golden Syrian hamster and Rhesus macaque
